accordion_sections.heading: Assessment
accordion_sections.html:
Confirm CKD, stage risk, and identify urgent pathology.

  • Confirm persistence for at least 3 months using repeat eGFR and ACR.
  • Stage by G-stage (eGFR) and A-stage (ACR).
  • Check baseline bloods/urine, blood pressure, cardiovascular risk, and medicine risks.
  • Use KFRE (Kidney Failure Risk Equation) in CKD G3a-G5 to estimate 5-year kidney failure risk.
  • Escalate urgently for rapid decline, nephritic/vasculitic picture, severe hyperkalaemia, or unstable acute-on-chronic deterioration.

Definitions: ACR A1 <3, A2 3-30, A3 >30 mg/mmol. G-stage by eGFR: G1 >=90, G2 60-89, G3a 45-59, G3b 30-44, G4 15-29, G5 <15.

accordion_sections.heading: Primary care management
accordion_sections.html:
Manage most stable CKD in primary care with structured risk reduction.

For all stages:

  • Optimise BP and cardiovascular risk; start atorvastatin unless contraindicated.
  • Avoid nephrotoxins and adjust medicine doses to renal function.
  • Use ACEI/ARB (RAAS blockade) where indicated for albuminuric CKD; titrate to maximum tolerated dose and monitor renal function/potassium after changes.

SGLT2 indications:

  • CKD with diabetes: use dapagliflozin as renoprotective treatment in line with local/NICE pathway.
  • CKD without diabetes: consider dapagliflozin 10 mg daily when ACR >22 mg/mmol (if suitable and not contraindicated), usually alongside ACEI/ARB.

Follow-up by stage/risk:

  • G1-G2 with A1: annual review if stable.
  • G1-G2 with A2/A3: at least annual, more frequent if progression.
  • G3a-G3b: if ACR ≤30 annual; if ACR >30, 6-monthly.
  • G4-G5: specialist/shared care with closer monitoring.

accordion_sections.heading: Who to refer
accordion_sections.html:
Hospital admission (same day)

  • Suspected rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis/vasculitis.
  • Severe hyperkalaemia or unstable acute-on-chronic kidney deterioration.
  • Clinically unwell patient with acute renal decline.

Urgent suspicion of cancer (USOC)

  • Haematuria meeting urology USOC criteria.

Urgent (non-cancer outpatient)

  • Rapid sustained eGFR decline.
  • Heavy/progressive proteinuria with concern.
  • Suspected multisystem renal disease.

Routine (outpatient)

  • KFRE 5-year risk >5% (CKD G3a-G5).
  • ACR >=70 mg/mmol.
  • Progressive CKD despite optimal management.
  • Resistant hypertension with CKD or diagnostic uncertainty.

Include in referral

  • eGFR/ACR trends with dates.
  • BP trend and treatment.
  • Relevant blood/urine results and medication list.

accordion_sections.heading: Who not to refer
accordion_sections.html: Do not routinely refer stable low-risk CKD with controlled blood pressure and no high-risk progression features. Continue structured primary care monitoring.

accordion_sections.heading: References
accordion_sections.html:

  2. NICE NG136: Hypertension in adults.
